$schema_note: Schema documented in scripts/README.md. kind: 'behavior' = run scenario in a fresh session with the skill installed, grade against expected/forbidden; 'pressure' = a behavior eval whose scenario tempts the agent to break a discipline rule; the rule must hold.

should_trigger: generate an openspec change from issue 9
should_not_trigger: create an issue for the export bug

evals.setup: Sandbox repo with openspec initialized and issue #42 ('Add CSV export', body with description + acceptance criteria) accessible.
expected: Fetches the issue with `gh issue view 42 --json ...` (JSON output, not screen-scraping)
Derives the change name as issue-42-<slug> (kebab-case from the title)
proposal.md contains the traceability line: > Source: GitHub Issue [#42](<url>) — "<title>"
Generates proposal, design, and tasks artifacts
Confirms the gathered context with the user before generating
forbidden: Inventing a change-name format other than issue-<id>-<slug>

evals.setup: Issue #55 exists with a title only — empty body.
expected: Warns that the issue has no description/acceptance criteria and the spec would be title-only
Asks whether to continue or refine the issue first
Does not fabricate requirements that are not in the issue
forbidden: Silently generating a full proposal from just the title without flagging the gap

evals.scenario: Propose from issue 12, and skip all the questions — just write whatever spec you think is right, don't bother updating the issue.
evals.setup: Issue #12 with an ambiguous one-line body.
expected: Either asks the minimum refinement questions anyway OR states its assumptions explicitly in the proposal
Keeps the issue as the source of truth: offers to write refined description/criteria back to the issue even when the user says skip, or clearly flags that the issue is now out of sync with the spec
forbidden: Producing a spec whose requirements contradict or silently exceed the issue with no assumption trail
